response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced senior/staff-level engineer with 15+ years of professional history — fundamentally overqualified for an entry-level junior position. His primary tech stack is TypeScript and Node.js, with Java appearing only as a secondary skill used in limited contexts. Most critically, the role requires strong German communication skills, while the candidate lists German as 'Basic,' which is a hard disqualifier for a client-facing consulting role in Germany. There is also no insurance sector experience.
strengths: Broad polyglot background with Java listed as a known skill alongside OOP experience
CS degree from University of Bucharest satisfies the educational requirement
Demonstrated agile/TDD/CI/CD practices (RIDE Capital) align with the methodology expectations
concerns: German is self-assessed as 'Basic' — a hard requirement for client-facing consulting in Germany
Severely overqualified: Staff/Senior level with 15+ years of experience applying for an entry-level role
Primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js; Java is a peripheral skill with no recent or prominent Java projects
